disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden complete loss of hearing in one ear beginning immediately after water entered it while swimming
trigger_question_examples: What happened just before the hearing loss started?
Can you hear anything at all on that side?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It came on the moment water got into my ear when I was swimming — now I can't hear anything at all on the right side.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe deep aural pressure and pain in the affected ear
trigger_question_examples: How bad is the pain and where exactly is it?
Is there a feeling of pressure inside the ear?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: There's this agonizing deep pressure inside the ear, like it's going to burst — it's the worst I've ever felt.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: New low-pitched roaring noise in the affected ear
trigger_question_examples: Any ringing or noises in the ear?
Any other new sensations in that ear?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, there's a loud low roaring sound in that ear that started with everything else.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No pus, no fluid discharge and no fever
trigger_question_examples: Is any fluid or pus draining from the ear?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, nothing is draining out of the ear and I haven't had a temperature.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Gently pulling on the outer ear does not sharply worsen the pain
trigger_question_examples: Does it hurt more if you gently tug your outer ear?
Is the outer ear or the opening tender to touch?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, tugging the outer ear doesn't really make the deep pain worse — the pain is deep inside.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Long-standing habit of cleaning the ears with cotton buds
trigger_question_examples: How do you usually clean your ears?
Do you ever use cotton buds in your ears?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've always used cotton buds to clean my ears, pretty much every day.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No spinning-room dizziness and no facial weakness or drooping
trigger_question_examples: Does the room feel like it's spinning?
Any weakness or drooping of your face?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, the room isn't spinning and my face feels normal — just off-balance from not hearing.
